threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you say 'the Overground' and still think it’s a type of coffee, that’s a dead giveaway. Real locals know it's anything but a caffeine fix.
Caught a midnight 59 last night. Two guys were trying to outdo each other with terrible accents. The bus driver just shook his head, barely amused.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the smell of fresh falafel wafts through the air on a Saturday morning, and you spot the familiar faces at the small market on Hermannstraße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that doubles as a thrift shop — buy a vintage jacket while sipping on a locally brewed IPA. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can spot the difference between a burrito at La Taqueria and one from Chipotle, even in the dark.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 this morning, and a guy was serenading everyone. When the driver joined in, you could feel the city’s heartbeat. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume parks like Parc Güell are the heart of Gràcia, but the real vibe pulses in the narrow streets of Travessera de Gràcia at dusk.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their struggle to order a simple ‘café con leche’ without mixing up the menus at the bar.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 10-minute walk is far. Everyone here knows that's just a warm-up before the coffee shop.
The 196 bus rolled in at 3 AM, but the driver was chatting with a couple of locals about lost dogs. They didn't even ask for fare.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a saxophonist jamming with a street artist, while a group of kids played football nearby—pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous street protests serving up free food. Just a slice of activism and community spirit that’s pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell by how they navigate the BART delays; natives roll with the punches, while newcomers just stare at their phones, confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a trumpet near the Civic Center Muni stop. He stopped mid-song as the bus driver yelled about delays, then picked right back up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the secret to Gràcia's charm is the parks. They miss the evenings when locals gather at the tiny bars, sharing tapas and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they order a 'café con leche' in the morning—far too loud and overly enthusiastic.
